title: Settings Page (`app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the main settings page for the authenticated user within the application's dashboard. It allows users to manage their profile information, including their avatar, name, email, and password. ## Purpose The primary purpose of this page is to provide a centralized location for users to manage their account settings. It's divided into two main tabs: 'Profile' and 'Billing'. The 'Profile' tab contains sections for updating personal details and security settings, while the 'Billing' tab is a placeholder for future payment management features. ## Key Exports and Components - `SettingsPage`: The main React component that renders the entire settings page. It uses state to manage the active tab and conditionally renders content based on the selected tab. ### Helper Components: - `Section`: A reusable layout component for organizing content within the settings page, featuring a title and description. - `Field`: A component for structuring form fields, displaying a label and the input element. - `Input`: A generic input field component with customizable types and event handling. - `StatusMessage`: Displays success or error messages to the user. - `SaveButton`: A button component for saving changes, with loading state indication. - `AvatarSection`: Handles the display and updating of the user's profile avatar. - `NameSection`: Allows users to update their first and last names. - `EmailSection`: Displays the user's verified primary email address. - `PasswordSection`: Enables users to change their account password, with validation for new passwords. - `DangerSection`: Contains actions that have significant consequences, such as signing out and deleting the account. - `TabButton`: Renders an interactive tab button for switching between different sections of the settings page. - `BillingPlaceholder`: A placeholder component for the future billing management section. ## Usage Example When a user navigates to the `/dashboard/settings` route, they will see the settings page. The 'Profile' tab is active by default, displaying sections for updating their avatar, name, email, password, and a danger zone for account actions. Users can switch to the 'Billing' tab to see a placeholder message indicating that payment management features are coming soon. ```tsx // Inside the SettingsPage component:
setTab("profile")} icon={User}> Profile setTab("billing")} icon={CreditCard}> Billing
{tab === "profile" && ( <> )} {tab === "billing" && }
```
